title: The battle against the MPEs disappointed me a bit in the EoE
display_title: The battle against the MPEs disappointed me a bit in the EoE
wikitext:
[]: The battle against the MPE's disappointed me a bit in the EoE             Quote Post by The Flying Fortress » Tue Jun 27, 2017 6:47 pm We have the logical answers ready for this, but I want to argue that this moment does not follow the usual battle style that the anime followed. Let's recap, remember some time when the EVA kicked the Angels' ass just as easily as Asuka did against the MPE's? In general, to defeat the Angels, it was necessary to build strategies and move a large contingent of Nerv's command staff and tower. We can see the human effort to defeat a single Angel even in the possession of three giant cyborgs. Even though they are now MPE's and not angels, should they theoretically not be stronger? They are copies of Eva with S2 engine. But then, we have this scene where Asuka defeats all MPE's easily, I really bother with it, yes, I know it's a movie and they need to do things in a hurry, but why does EVA-02 defeat them that way? It seems to me that they tried to do a private show of a character here and ignored the potential of combat that the MPE could have. Also, you can not find animation of this strange scene? , Like EVA-02 flying (Dufuq?) And also when she grabs one of the MPE's by the neck seems to be a bar fight.

[]: Re: The battle against the MPE's disappointed me a bit in the EoE             Quote Post by Cybermat47 » Tue Jun 27, 2017 10:47 pm But Asuka really didn't defeat the Evas that easily. She didn't defeat them at all. None of the damage she did actually mattered in the end.

[]: Re: The battle against the MPE's disappointed me a bit in the EoE             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Jun 28, 2017 1:40 am The Flying Fortress wrote: Even though they are now MPE's and not angels, should they theoretically not be stronger? They are copies of Eva with S2 engine.

[]: Re: The battle against the MPE's disappointed me a bit in the EoE             Quote Post by The Flying Fortress » Wed Jun 28, 2017 7:47 pm pwhodges wrote: Which is why they won - they could recover from injuries and keep going whereas Unit-02 couldn't.

[]: Re: The battle against the MPE's disappointed me a bit in the EoE             Quote Post by silvermoonlight » Thu Jun 29, 2017 7:20 am Cybermat47 wrote: But Asuka really didn't defeat the Evas that easily. She didn't defeat them at all. None of the damage she did actually mattered in the end.

[]: Re: The battle against the MPE's disappointed me a bit in the EoE             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jun 29, 2017 9:17 am silvermoonlight : None of their cores were destroyed, so none of them were actually dead. Being piloted by dummy plugs instead of people, the harpies were freed from any considerations of pain. But speaking of that, I do find it absolutely inane that they bother attacking in the first place, only to get trashed, play possum, and then, only once Eva-02's energy is out, get nasty. One's confronted with any number of weird questions about the sequence. (A) If they were always that nasty, why did they wait until after they'd been mutilated to show it? (B) On a similar note, they show exceptional coordinating ability from the point that Eva-02 is down and onward. If that ability to coordinate their efforts to perfection was always there, why the fuck wait? (C) If, for whatever reason, the harpies' ability to coordinate doesn't work against a moving target, then why didn't Seele just wait until Eva-02's power out to deploy them? (D) Scratch that -- why didn't Seele just wait until Eva-02 was down to deploy the MPs, period? Seriously, what was Eva-02 going to do to affect their plans before her battery ran out? Nothing, pretty much. On the other hand, if they throw their Instrumentality Task Force directly at her, they introduce the possibility that she might wreck them and their plans. I could go on and on. There are so many aspects of the encounter that are just absolutely nonsensical and I DON'T CARE HOW MUCH PEOPLE LIKE IT, IT BUGS ME .

[]: Re: The battle against the MPE's disappointed me a bit in the EoE             Quote Post by silvermoonlight » Thu Jun 29, 2017 9:45 am Reichu wrote: silvermoonlight : None of their cores were destroyed, so none of them were actually dead. Being piloted by dummy plugs instead of people, the harpies were freed from any considerations of pain.

[]: Re: The battle against the MPE's disappointed me a bit in the EoE             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Thu Jun 29, 2017 10:01 am silvermoonlight wrote: Good point I had not considered that but then that begs the other question why not smash up there cores? She knows where the core is she proberbly saw it on 01 when it was being repaired and Shinji was sucked in to the core.

[]: Re: The battle against the MPE's disappointed me a bit in the EoE             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Jun 29, 2017 10:03 am silvermoonlight wrote: Good point I had not considered that but then that begs the other question why not smash up there cores?

[]: Re: The battle against the MPE's disappointed me a bit in the EoE             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jun 29, 2017 10:10 am silvermoonlight wrote: why not smash up there cores?

[]: Re: The battle against the MPE's disappointed me a bit in the EoE             Quote Post by CommanderFish » Thu Jun 29, 2017 9:25 pm Reichu wrote: why didn't Seele just wait until Eva-02 was down to deploy the MPs, period? Seriously, what was Eva-02 going to do to affect their plans before her battery ran out? Nothing, pretty much. On the other hand, if they throw their Instrumentality Task Force directly at her, they introduce the possibility that she might wreck them and their plans.

[]: Re: The battle against the MPE's disappointed me a bit in the EoE             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jun 29, 2017 9:55 pm CommanderFish wrote: I think this makes a lot more sense if you look at the MPE's as being like vultures.

[]: Re: The battle against the MPE's disappointed me a bit in the EoE             Quote Post by CommanderFish » Fri Jun 30, 2017 12:12 am Reichu wrote: Vultures don't throw themselves at prey that's still quite alive and kicking -- they wait until it's safe to come in and feast. For the comparison to hold water in a more than purely superficial sense, the Eva Series would have needed to remain circling at the beginning until Eva-02 was immobile.

[]: Re: The battle against the MPE's disappointed me a bit in the EoE             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Jun 30, 2017 2:57 am Reichu wrote: Maya had been keeping track of the enemy Evas' activity, and she doesn't say anything about the "fallen Eva Series" reactivating until after Eva-02 gpes down. That rules out one of MPs throwing the Spear, meaning it probably threw itself -- foreshadowing the autonomous return of the original Spear later on.

[]: Re: The battle against the MPE's disappointed me a bit in the EoE             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jun 30, 2017 10:29 am pwhodges wrote: She's not necessarily a perfect observer, though, so that conclusion is not certain.

Let's recap, remember some time when the EVA kicked the Angels' ass just as easily as Asuka did against the MPE's? In general, to defeat the Angels, it was nece
